title: rosa woodsii var. terrens
intro: Rosa woodsii var. terrens, also known as the Western Wood's rose, is a fragrant, deciduous shrub native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its pink to purple flowers and thorny stems. The medicinal properties of this plant include its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Rosa woodsii var. terrens include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for soothing digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Rosa woodsii var. terrens include tea infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
